name: Atlas Graph Redesign
outcome_goal: Atlas becomes a stable, professional, high-density knowledge graph and structured graph-table interface backed by authoritative definition/object hierarchy data.
anti_requirements: Do not create a parallel graph authority outside existing API/CQRS/data-dictionary surfaces.
Do not keep exploding force-graph behavior.
Do not use a decorative one-hue palette or purely atmospheric styling.
Do not hand-roll a new persistence model for Atlas nodes.

jobs.label: discover_atlas_authority
jobs.prompt: Inspect Atlas UI components, graph data API routes, operator graph/query surfaces, data dictionary/object hierarchy sources, and existing table/list patterns. Identify the authoritative read model Atlas should consume.

jobs.label: stabilize_atlas_graph_layout
jobs.prompt: Replace unstable/exploding graph behavior with deterministic, bounded layout defaults. Dragging should pin or anchor nodes predictably. Simulation must settle quickly and remain inspectable at high density.

jobs.label: apply_atlas_industrial_visual_system
jobs.prompt: Apply a professional industrial graph aesthetic: dense labels, restrained contrast, object-type encoding, clear selection states, and no decorative glow/orb palette. Keep text legible and non-overlapping.

jobs.label: integrate_data_dictionary_hierarchy
jobs.prompt: Map Atlas nodes to the authoritative definition/object hierarchy exposed by existing data dictionary or operator graph surfaces. Display object kind, authority source, definition summary, and parent/child relationship when available.

jobs.label: add_atlas_hybrid_table_view
jobs.prompt: Add a toggle between spatial graph and graph-based table view. Table view must support structured relational management: source node, relation, target node, object kind, authority source, and selection sync with graph.

jobs.label: test_atlas_redesign
jobs.prompt: Add tests for stable layout inputs, node hierarchy fields, graph/table toggle, row selection sync, and no exploding physics regression. Include a Playwright or component-render smoke if Atlas currently has visual test infrastructure.
